notes/0/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother reported that child is starting junior kindergarten in the fall and is “hard to understand” for unfamiliar adults. No history of hearing concerns. She notices that he often leaves off sounds at the ends of words and says “doo” for “zoo.” No major changes since initial assessment.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned to the therapy room with some hesitation but separated from caregiver with reassurance. Once settled, he was curious and engaged in play-based activities (cars, farm set). Attention span was variable; needed occasional redirection back to speech tasks but responded well to visual supports and short activities.

Goals / session focus:
1) Final consonant production (CVC words: /p, t, m/)
- Practiced final consonants in simple words (e.g., “cup, cap, cat, hat, map, mom”) at the imitated word level.
- Child frequently omitted the final sound or replaced it with a neutral vowel (e.g., “cu-,” “ma-”).
- With simultaneous production and visual cueing (pointing to mouth, touching picture of “end sound”), he was able to mark some final sounds, but consistency was low and required maximal modeling.

2) Initial /k/ in syllables and simple words
- Targeted /k/ in isolation and CV syllables (“ku, ki, ka”) using a “back sound” cue (tongue up and back).
- Child often substituted /t/ for /k/ in words (e.g., “tar” for “car”).
- With hand-under-chin cue and mirror, he could approximate /k/ in isolation in several attempts but had difficulty carrying this into words without full modeling.

3) Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Introduced “snake sound” /s/ in isolation and in simple syllables (“see, soo”).
- Child produced a mix of /d/ and /th/ substitutions, with occasional accurate /s/ when watching clinician’s mouth closely.
- Required frequent verbal reminders (“keep your tongue behind your teeth, blow air”) and benefited from tactile cue on the sides of his mouth.

4) Functional communication / speech sample
- Collected brief language sample during play. Child used mainly 2–3 word combinations (“my car,” “more bubbles,” “I want that”).
- Intelligibility decreased as utterances got longer; caregiver often interpreted for clinician.

Plan / recommendations:
- Continue to target final consonant production at CVC word level with maximal supports.
- Maintain focus on establishing consistent /k/ and /s/ in isolation and simple syllables before moving to more complex words.

notes/1/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child entered the room more readily today and appeared comfortable with clinician. He was eager to choose toys and participated well in structured play. Some silliness when tasks became more challenging, but responded to visual schedule and turn-taking games.

Goals / session focus:
1) Final consonant production in CVC words (/p, t, m/)
- Revisited final consonants at imitated word level using picture cards and a simple “end sound” game.
- Compared to last week, child more consistently attempted to close words with a sound, though accuracy remained variable.
- With moderate verbal and visual cues (pointing to end of word on card, pausing before final sound), he produced clearer final /p/ and /t/ in several trials, with fewer complete omissions.

2) Initial /k/ in CV and CVC words
- Practiced /k/ in CV syllables (“ku, ki, ko”) and expanded to a small set of words (e.g., “car, key, cow”).
- Child produced more accurate /k/ in isolation and syllables than previous session with less need for simultaneous production.
- In words, he still showed frequent /t/ substitutions but demonstrated a few independent correct /k/ productions when given a “back sound” reminder only.

3) Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Continued work on “snake sound” /s/ with mirror and visual cue (snake picture).
- Child showed slightly increased accuracy in isolation, sustaining a clearer /s/ for longer durations.
- In simple syllables (“see, soo”), he was able to imitate /s/ in a small number of trials with close modeling; still required maximal cueing for tongue placement.

4) Functional play-based speech
- During play, child used short phrases (2–3 words) spontaneously (e.g., “my turn,” “more bubbles,” “car go”).
- Final consonants were still frequently omitted in spontaneous speech, but occasional marked endings were noted when clinician modeled just prior.

Plan / recommendations:
- Continue current targets, as skills are just beginning to emerge and benefit from repetition.
- Next session, consider introducing a simple final consonant “listening” game to build awareness of when words have an ending sound.
- Home practice: caregiver to continue slow, emphasized final sounds in familiar words, and play a simple “same or different” game (e.g., “cu” vs. “cup”) to highlight endings without expecting perfect repetition.
notes/2/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned easily and appeared excited to show clinician his toy car. He maintained attention for longer stretches this session and was more willing to repeat words when prompted. Minimal avoidance; responded well to praise and sticker rewards.

Goals / session focus:
1) Final consonant production in CVC words (/p, t, m/; imitation and early cued independence)
- Used picture cards and a simple board game to elicit CVC words (e.g., “cup, cap, cat, map, mat, mop”).
- Child showed a noticeable increase in attempts to close words with final sounds, with fewer complete omissions.
- With moderate verbal cues and occasional visual prompts, he produced clearer final /p/ and /t/ in many trials, and began to self-correct when he heard his own omissions.
- Began to trial a few productions with reduced cueing; he was sometimes able to produce final /p/ independently after one modeled attempt.

2) Initial /k/ at word level
- Targeted /k/ in initial position of familiar words (“car, key, cow, cookie”) within a car-race game.
- Child produced more accurate /k/ in imitation, needing fewer simultaneous productions and less tactile support.
- Showed several instances of independent /k/ in words after a brief reminder to “use your back sound,” indicating improved motor planning for this phoneme.

3) Initial /s/ in syllables and early word level
- Practiced /s/ in CV syllables and moved into a small set of words (“sun, soap, sock”) at the imitated word level.
- Child demonstrated improved ability to sustain /s/ in isolation and was more consistent in shaping /s/ rather than /d/ or /th/.
- At word level, he still required high levels of support (mirror, verbal placement cues), but there were more clearly correct productions than in previous sessions.

4) Functional carryover of final consonants
- During structured play, clinician modeled short phrases with final consonants (e.g., “big cup,” “hot dog”).
- Child occasionally carried over final /p/ and /t/ into short phrases when immediately modeled, suggesting early generalization from single words to simple combinations.

Plan / recommendations:
- Given emerging accuracy, continue CVC final consonant work at word level and gradually fade cueing when possible.
- For /k/, begin to trial short carrier phrases (e.g., “my car,” “go cow”) in upcoming sessions if word-level accuracy remains strong.
- For /s/, continue to solidify isolation and syllable-level production before expanding word lists.
- Home practice: caregiver to practice 5–8 CVC words with child daily, focusing on clear end sounds, using picture cards or objects at home (e.g., cup, hat, bed, map).
notes/3/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child entered session with good energy and quickly engaged with the chosen game. Attention was sustained for structured tasks with brief movement breaks. He showed pride when he produced words accurately and occasionally requested, “my turn more,” indicating good engagement.

Goals / session focus:
1) Final consonants in CVC words (/p, t, m, n/; imitation → cued independence)
- Expanded final consonant work to include /n/ in addition to /p, t, m/ using picture cards and a simple “feed the puppet” game.
- Child demonstrated clearer and more consistent marking of final consonants compared to previous sessions, particularly for /p/ and /t/.
- With moderate cues, he produced many accurate CVC words; in some familiar items (e.g., “cup, cat, map”), he required only a light verbal reminder or none at all, indicating readiness to begin fading supports.

2) Initial /k/ in words and short carrier phrases
- Practiced initial /k/ words (“car, key, cow, cookie”) and introduced short carrier phrases (e.g., “my car,” “big cow”) at the imitated level.
- Child maintained improved /k/ accuracy at word level with less cueing and was able to produce several correct /k/ sounds within short phrases when modeled.
- Some reversion to /t/ was observed when phrases became longer, but he responded to brief reminders to “use your back sound.”

3) Initial /s/ at word level
- Continued work on /s/ in words (“sun, soap, sock, soup”) using a “snake sound” visual and mirror.
- Child produced a greater proportion of accurate /s/ in imitation, although substitutions (/d/, /th/) still occurred.
- He began to accept fewer tactile cues and responded better to simple verbal prompts and visual supports, showing moderate improvement in consistency.

4) Early phrase-level practice with final consonants
- Introduced short 2–3 word phrases that combined final consonants with high-interest nouns (e.g., “big cup,” “hot soup”) in imitation.
- Child was able to mark some final sounds within these phrases when provided with a clear model and slowed rate, though accuracy was lower than at single-word level.

Plan / recommendations:
- Continue to consolidate CVC final consonant accuracy; if consistency remains high, gradually increase the number of phrase-level trials next session.
- Maintain initial /k/ practice in short carrier phrases and begin to increase variety of /k/ words.
- Continue structured /s/ word practice; may trial limited /s/ blends in future once single /s/ is more stable.
- Home practice: caregiver to model and practice 2-word phrases with final consonants (e.g., “big cup,” “red hat”) during daily routines, focusing on slow, clear speech.

notes/2/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother reported that child is now sometimes correcting themselves when saying /s/ words (e.g., “dun…sun”).
- She has noticed clearer speech in a few familiar words but still finds it hard to understand full sentences.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child separated from caregiver without difficulty and quickly engaged with tabletop activities.
- Demonstrated good turn-taking and was highly motivated by sticker rewards.
- Needed occasional breaks between drill tasks; responded well to alternating structured work with play.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ at word and emerging phrase level
- Practiced initial /s/ words (“sun,” “sock,” “soap,” “sand,” “see”) in imitation and then in a simple game (e.g., feeding pictures to a puppet).
- Accuracy at imitated word level was noticeably higher than last week; only occasional substitutions to /d/.
- Introduced short carrier phrases (“my sun,” “see sock”) with models. Child was able to imitate several 2-word combinations with reasonably clear /s/ when cued to slow down.

2. Initial /f/ at word level (imitation)
- Targeted /f/ in words such as “feet,” “fish,” “phone,” “fun.”
- Child used correct lip and teeth placement more consistently; fewer productions were stopped to /p/.
- Some independent attempts to say /f/ words during play (e.g., “fish”) without prompting, though accuracy varied.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words (/p, m, t/)
- Expanded targets to include final /t/ (e.g., “hat,” “boat”) while maintaining /p/ and /m/ from previous sessions.
- In imitation, child produced final consonants with moderate success, especially when SLP used a gesture to mark the end sound.
- In spontaneous play, final consonants were still often omitted, but a few spontaneous accurate productions were noted.

4. Short phrases with mixed targets
- Used a picture scene to encourage short phrases like “see fish,” “big hat,” “my mom.”
- Child imitated 2–3-word phrases with moderate support, showing better clarity on practiced target words than on unfamiliar vocabulary.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Progress suggests readiness to continue building phrase-level practice for initial /s/ and /f/ in structured tasks.
- Plan to further increase opportunities for short carrier phrases and begin to fade some cues when possible.
- Caregiver encouraged to model slow, clear speech during daily routines and highlight a few practice words (e.g., “sun,” “fish,” “feet”) in simple phrases such as “see sun,” “big fish.”
notes/3/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother shared that child is “using the snake sound more” and that grandparents commented that speech sounded clearer this week.
- She reports that the child likes practicing “fish” and “feet” at bath time.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child arrived in good spirits and quickly joined the session.
- Sustained attention well for short drill activities and followed simple directions in play.
- Mild silliness when tasks became repetitive, but responded to changes in activity and visual schedules.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ at word and carrier phrase level
- Continued work on initial /s/ words (“sun,” “sock,” “sand,” “soap,” “sit”).
- At imitated word level, productions were more consistent, with fewer errors and less need for intensive cueing.
- Child successfully imitated short carrier phrases such as “I see sun,” “my sock,” and “see soap” with increasing independence.

2. Initial /f/ at word and emerging phrase level
- Practiced /f/ words (“fish,” “feet,” “food,” “fun”) in a fishing game.
- Child produced /f/ accurately in imitation for many trials and started to use some of these words spontaneously during play (e.g., “fish” without direct model).
- Introduced short phrases like “my fish,” “feet up,” which child imitated with moderate support.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words (/p, m, t/)
- Revisited final /p, m, t/ in words (e.g., “cup,” “mom,” “hat,” “boat”).
- In structured imitation, child showed similar or slightly improved consistency, especially when SLP emphasized the final sound.
- Final consonants remained less consistent in spontaneous speech, though a few self-corrections were observed.

4. Three-word phrases incorporating target words (imitation)
- Modeled short three-word phrases (e.g., “I see sun,” “I want fish,” “big red hat”).
- Child could imitate several three-word phrases, but speech clarity decreased as length increased, with some omissions of final sounds and reduced accuracy on less familiar words.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Continue to build phrase-level practice for /s/ and /f/ and gradually increase length of utterances as tolerated.
- Maintain focus on final consonants in structured tasks, with emphasis on self-monitoring.
- Home practice: encourage child to use target words in simple phrases during daily routines (e.g., “my sock,” “see sun,” “my fish”) and praise attempts.
notes/4/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother reported that speech has been “about the same” this week. Child continues to enjoy practicing specific words but does not always carry them over in everyday talking.
- No new concerns or changes at home.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child initially appeared somewhat tired but warmed up with a preferred game.
- Participated in structured tasks with encouragement; needed occasional redirection when trying to skip to preferred toys.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ in words and short phrases
- Practiced initial /s/ words from previous sessions and added a few new ones (e.g., “sock,” “soap,” “sun,” “sand,” “soup”).
- Accuracy at the imitated word level was similar to last week; child continued to need verbal reminders for tongue placement.
- In short carrier phrases (e.g., “I see sun,” “my sock”), performance remained generally consistent, with a mix of accurate productions and occasional substitutions.

2. Initial /f/ in words and short phrases
- Targeted /f/ in words (“fish,” “feet,” “food,” “fun”) within a turn-taking game.
- Child maintained previous gains in imitation, with clear /f/ in many attempts but inconsistent spontaneous use.
- Short phrases (e.g., “my fish,” “feet up”) were imitated with similar accuracy to last week, requiring moderate cueing.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words
- Repeated practice with final /p, m, t/ in familiar words (e.g., “cup,” “mom,” “hat,” “boat”).
- Accuracy in structured imitation appeared stable; no major change from prior session.
- Final consonants were still often dropped in more spontaneous speech.

4. Phrase length and intelligibility
- Encouraged 2–3-word phrases in play (e.g., “more bubbles please,” “I see fish”).
- Child attempted several longer phrases, but speech clarity decreased with added length; intelligibility remained best in short, practiced combinations.

Plan/Recommendations:
- This week appeared to be a consolidation period with skills holding steady.
- Plan to continue with similar targets next session, with a focus on building consistency and gently encouraging more independent productions.
- Home practice: continue using simple, repeated phrases around daily routines (e.g., “my sock,” “big cup,” “I see ___”) and model clear final sounds without pressuring the child.
notes/5/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother noted “a few more clear words” this week, particularly “sun” and “fish,” and stated that the child sometimes repeats phrases heard in therapy.
- No concerns about hearing or overall health.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child transitioned smoothly into the session and appeared more talkative today.
- Demonstrated good engagement during games that incorporated movement and visuals.
- Responded well to praise and seemed proud of successful attempts.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ in words and simple phrases, with increased independence
- Continued practice of initial /s/ words and short phrases (e.g., “see sun,” “my sock,” “soap here”).
- Child showed slightly increased independence, occasionally producing clear /s/ in familiar words without a direct model.
- Needed fewer direct prompts to use the correct tongue placement, though reminders were still beneficial.

2. Initial /f/ in words and phrases
- Focused on /f/ in words (“fish,” “feet,” “funny,” “food”) within a fishing and feeding game.
- Child demonstrated more consistent /f/ productions in imitation and began to independently say “fish” and “feet” appropriately during play.
- Short phrases (e.g., “my fish,” “feet on”) were imitated with moderate cueing and were somewhat clearer than previous sessions.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and short phrases
- Revisited final /p, m, t/ in words (“cup,” “mom,” “hat,” “boat”) and embedded them in short phrases (e.g., “big cup,” “my hat”).
- Child continued to require modeling for final sounds, but there were a few more instances of self-correction when cued to “check the end.”

4. 2–3-word phrases with target sounds
- Used picture scenes to elicit short phrases such as “I see sun,” “big fish,” “my hat on.”
- Child attempted several phrases; accuracy was best on practiced target words, with some reductions on connecting words.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Given emerging independence with /s/ and /f/ in familiar words, plan to gradually increase opportunities for spontaneous use in structured play next session.
- Continue supporting final consonant use with visual and gestural cues.
- Home program: caregiver encouraged to pause briefly after modeling target phrases (e.g., “I see sun”) to allow the child a chance to repeat or attempt independently.
notes/6/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother reported that child is now using “sun” and “fish” spontaneously at home and that siblings are starting to understand these words more easily.
- She also mentioned that the child attempts short phrases but can still be hard to understand when excited.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child was cheerful and initiated interaction with the clinician.
- Maintained good attention in both table-top and floor activities, especially when given choices about games.
- Showed increased willingness to try again after an unclear production.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ at word and emerging spontaneous phrase level
- Practiced initial /s/ words (“sun,” “sock,” “soap,” “sit,” “sand”) in a picture sorting game.
- Child produced /s/ in imitation with good consistency and required fewer verbal reminders.
- During play, began to use some /s/ words spontaneously (e.g., “sun,” “sit”) with clearer articulation; occasional self-corrections were noted.

2. Initial /f/ at word and phrase level
- Targeted /f/ in words and phrases (e.g., “fish,” “feet,” “fun,” “my fish,” “feet up”) during a pretend play routine.
- Child demonstrated increasing accuracy in both imitation and prompted spontaneous use, particularly with familiar words.
- Needed minimal cueing to maintain correct lip and teeth placement.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and short phrases
- Continued work on final /p, m, t/ in words (“cup,” “mom,” “hat,” “boat”) and phrases (“big cup,” “my hat on”).
- Accuracy in structured imitation was similar to last week, but child showed more frequent attempts to include final consonants when reminded to “finish the word.”

4. 2–3-word phrases with mixed targets
- Encouraged phrases such as “I see fish,” “big sun up,” “my mom hat.”
- Child imitated multiple phrases with improved clarity on targeted words; errors were more often on less familiar vocabulary or function words.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Moderate progress supports continued focus on increasing spontaneous use of accurate /s/ and /f/ in play.
- Plan to add a few new /s/ and /f/ words next session while maintaining work on final consonants.
- Home practice: caregiver encouraged to highlight and praise clear /s/ and /f/ words and gently model final sounds in everyday routines (e.g., “cup,” “hat”).
notes/7/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother shared that child had a “really good speech week,” with more understandable words and short phrases like “my fish” and “see sun.”
- Family members are noticing clearer speech and are encouraging the child to “show” their new words.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child entered session independently and immediately engaged with materials.
- Demonstrated strong motivation to communicate, frequently initiating comments during play.
- Tolerated brief periods of more structured practice without frustration.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ at phrase and early sentence level
- Practiced initial /s/ in 2–3-word phrases and short sentences (e.g., “I see sun,” “my sock is wet,” “see soap here”).
- Child produced /s/ with good consistency in imitation and began to use several of these phrases spontaneously in structured play.
- Required only occasional reminders for correct placement, indicating a meaningful increase in independence.

2. Initial /f/ at phrase and short sentence level
- Targeted /f/ in phrases and simple sentences (e.g., “my fish is big,” “feet on bed,” “I see food”).
- Child showed improved carryover of accurate /f/ into longer utterances, especially for familiar words like “fish” and “feet.”
- Some spontaneous comments included clear /f/ (e.g., “funny fish”) with minimal cueing.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and phrases
- Continued work on final /p, m, t/ in words and short phrases (“big cup,” “mom up,” “hat on top”).
- Child produced final consonants more consistently in imitation and occasionally in prompted spontaneous phrases.
- Still tends to omit final consonants in fast, excited speech, but shows better awareness when asked to “check the end.”

4. Short functional sentences with target words
- Used picture scenes and role-play to elicit simple sentences such as “I see the sun,” “The fish is in,” “My hat is on.”
- Child could imitate these with moderate clarity; errors tended to cluster on non-target words, while the target words themselves were often produced more accurately.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Given the noticeable improvement and increased independence, it is appropriate to continue expanding phrase and short sentence work with /s/ and /f/.
- Plan to gradually increase complexity of contexts while monitoring intelligibility.
- Home program: encourage child to use short, functional sentences with target words during daily routines (e.g., “I see sun,” “my feet are wet”) and continue positive reinforcement for clear productions.
notes/8/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother reported that child’s speech “seems about the same as last week,” with ongoing clear productions of favorite words and phrases.
- She noted that clarity decreases when the child is tired or upset.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child transitioned well to the therapy space and engaged in familiar routines.
- Attention and participation were generally good, though child requested more frequent breaks near the end of the session.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ in phrases and short sentences
- Practiced previously learned phrases and simple sentences with /s/ (e.g., “I see sun,” “my sock is wet,” “see soap here”).
- Accuracy and independence appeared similar to last week; child continued to produce /s/ clearly in many structured opportunities.
- Occasional errors occurred when speaking quickly or using less familiar vocabulary.

2. Initial /f/ in phrases and short sentences
- Targeted /f/ in phrases and sentences (e.g., “my fish is big,” “feet on bed,” “funny food”).
- Child maintained previous gains, showing consistent /f/ production in familiar words, particularly in structured activities.
- Spontaneous use of /f/ words was observed during play, though not yet consistent across all contexts.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and phrases
- Continued practice with final /p, m, t/ in words and phrases (e.g., “big cup,” “my hat,” “mom up”).
- Performance remained stable, with better accuracy in imitation than in spontaneous speech.
- Child responded to reminders to “finish the word,” but still required frequent cueing.

4. Short functional sentences with mixed targets
- Elicited simple sentences containing both /s/ and /f/ (e.g., “I see fish,” “The sun is up,” “My feet are in”).
- Child imitated these sentences with similar clarity to previous session, with target sounds often produced more accurately than connecting words.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Skills appear stable this week, indicating consolidation at the current level.
- Plan to maintain similar tasks next session, with a focus on building consistency and slowly increasing demands only as tolerated.
- Home practice: caregiver encouraged to continue modeling and practicing a small set of functional sentences (e.g., “I see sun,” “My feet are wet”) and to reduce speed of speech when the child appears rushed.
notes/9/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother indicated that speech continues to be “pretty steady,” with clear productions of some practiced phrases but variable clarity in everyday conversation.
- No new medical or developmental concerns were reported.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child was cooperative and followed familiar routines without difficulty.
- Attention was adequate for short drills and play-based practice; needed occasional prompts to stay on task when activities became less novel.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ in phrases and short sentences
- Revisited known /s/ phrases and sentences (e.g., “I see sun,” “my sock is on,” “see soap here”).
- Accuracy and independence were similar to the prior session; child consistently produced /s/ correctly in many practiced contexts.
- Some reductions in clarity noted when longer sentences were attempted, mainly affecting non-target words.

2. Initial /f/ in phrases and short sentences
- Continued work with /f/ words and phrases (“my fish is big,” “feet on bed,” “funny food”).
- Child maintained good accuracy with /f/ in familiar words in structured activities.
- Spontaneous use of /f/ remained present but inconsistent in conversation.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and phrases
- Ongoing practice with final /p, m, t/ in CVC words and simple phrases (“big cup,” “hat on top,” “mom up”).
- No major change from previous week; child continued to produce final consonants more reliably with direct modeling than independently.

4. Short functional sentences integrating multiple targets
- Elicited sentences containing both /s/ and /f/ (e.g., “I see fish,” “The sun is up,” “My feet are wet”).
- Child imitated these with generally similar clarity to last session; target sounds were typically clearer than the rest of the sentence.

Plan/Recommendations:
- This appears to be another consolidation week with stable performance.
- Plan to continue at current level, gradually adding variety of vocabulary while maintaining supports for accuracy.
- Home program: encourage child to use practiced sentences in natural routines (e.g., bath time, getting dressed) and provide calm, clear models when speech becomes rushed or less intelligible.
notes/10/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother reported that child continues to use several clear words and short phrases with /s/ and /f/ at home, but overall intelligibility in longer speech remains variable.
- She feels speech is “about the same” as the last couple of weeks.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child transitioned easily and participated in familiar routines.
- Attention and cooperation were good for structured games; mild distractibility noted toward the end of the session but easily redirected.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ in phrases and short sentences
- Practiced /s/ in known phrases and sentences (e.g., “I see sun,” “my sock is wet,” “see soap there”).
- Performance was consistent with previous sessions, with accurate /s/ in many practiced contexts and occasional errors when speech rate increased.

2. Initial /f/ in phrases and short sentences
- Continued work on /f/ in words and phrases (“my fish is big,” “feet on bed,” “funny food”).
- Child maintained clear /f/ productions in structured tasks; no notable increase or decrease in accuracy.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and phrases
- Ongoing practice with final /p, m, t/ in CVC words and simple phrases (“big cup,” “mom up,” “hat on top”).
- Accuracy remained stable in imitation, with final consonant omissions persisting in more spontaneous speech.

4. Functional sentence production with target sounds

notes/11/note_text:
Parent/Caregiver Report:
- Mother indicated that speech is similar to recent weeks: clear on practiced words and short phrases, but less clear in fast, excited conversation.
- She reports that family members can now understand several of the child’s favorite phrases.

Behaviour/Engagement:
- Child entered the room willingly and quickly engaged with preferred toys.
- Demonstrated consistent participation in structured and play-based activities, with occasional requests to repeat favorite games.

Goals/Activities:
1. Initial /s/ in phrases and short sentences
- Reviewed familiar /s/ phrases and short sentences (e.g., “I see sun,” “my sock is on,” “see soap here”).
- Accuracy remained steady, with reliable production of /s/ in many structured contexts and some carryover into spontaneous comments during play.

2. Initial /f/ in phrases and short sentences
- Continued practice of /f/ in words and sentences (“my fish is big,” “feet on bed,” “funny food”).
- Child maintained good accuracy with /f/ in known words; no major shifts from previous sessions.

3. Final consonant production in CVC words and phrases
- Repeated work on final /p, m, t/ in CVC words and short phrases (“big cup,” “mom up,” “hat on top”).
- Final consonants were present more often in imitation than in spontaneous speech; overall pattern consistent with recent weeks.

4. Short functional sentences combining target sounds
- Elicited sentences such as “I see fish,” “The sun is up,” and “My feet are wet” using picture supports.
- Child imitated these with similar clarity to prior sessions, with best accuracy on the targeted words.

Plan/Recommendations:
- Performance this week is consistent with the recent pattern of stable skills at the current level.
- Recommend ongoing therapy to support further generalization of accurate /s/ and /f/ productions and improved use of final consonants in spontaneous speech.
- Home program: continue modeling and gently eliciting practiced sentences during daily routines, and encourage the child to slow down and “finish words” when communication breakdowns occur.

notes/0/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver shared that they are most concerned about child's speech clarity, especially with new listeners. Noted that child often leaves off sounds at the ends of words and sometimes "mushes" words together. No major changes since initial evaluation.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned with some hesitation but separated from caregiver with reassurance. Once in the room, child was curious and engaged with toys. Attention to adult-directed tasks was variable; needed frequent reminders to "listen" and return to table tasks but responded well to short, play-based activities.

Goals / Observations:
1. Syllable shapes (CV, CVCV)
- Targeted early consonants /m, b, p/ in simple syllables (e.g., "ma," "bee," "boo," "mama," "baby"). Child imitated CV shapes fairly consistently, though productions were sometimes quiet or slightly distorted. CVCV forms were more effortful; child tended to reduce to one syllable (e.g., "mama" → "ma"). Moderate verbal models and visual cues were needed.

2. Final consonant production (CVC words)
- Introduced final /p, t, m/ at imitated word level (e.g., "cup," "cat," "ham"). Child frequently omitted final sounds or added a schwa ("cup" → "cuh"). With direct modeling and visual cues (tapping for end sound), child produced a few clearer final consonants, but accuracy remained low and inconsistent.

3. Initial /k/ in isolation and syllables
- Practiced back-of-the-tongue sound /k/ in isolation using mirror and tongue-back cues. Child could approximate /k/ in isolation intermittently but often substituted /t/. In CV syllables ("koo," "kee"), substitutions persisted. Tactile and verbal prompts were required for most trials.

4. Early phrase production for clarity (2–3 word combinations)
- In play, child used many 2–3 word phrases, but several words within the phrase were not clearly articulated. SLP modeled slower, slightly exaggerated productions (e.g., "big ball," "my car"). Child occasionally imitated clearer versions with direct imitation but did not yet carry this over independently.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Continue focusing on establishing consistent final consonants and more accurate /k/ in isolation and simple syllables. Maintain practice of CV and CVCV syllable shapes with early consonants.
- Caregiver encouraged to model slow, clear speech at home and emphasize end sounds in a natural way (e.g., gently highlighting the last sound in words like "cup" and "hat").
- Plan to reassess response to /k/ cues next session and consider introducing a small set of familiar CVC words if accuracy in isolation improves.
notes/1/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver reported child is "chatty" at home but still difficult to understand, especially when excited. Noticed a few clearer words this week ("cup," "mommy") when they reminded child to "say it slowly." No other changes.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child separated easily and was eager to show toys from the waiting room. Participation was generally good with brief periods of silliness. Responded well to visual schedules and short movement breaks.

Goals / Observations:
1. Syllable shapes (CV, CVCV)
- Continued work on CV and CVCV sequences with /m, b, p, t, d/. Child showed slightly better consistency with CVCV ("mama," "baby," "puppy"), producing both syllables more often rather than reducing to one. Still required direct modeling, but fewer repetitions were needed for an accurate attempt.

2. Final consonant production (CVC words)
- Revisited final /p, t, m/ in simple CVC words. Child demonstrated more frequent attempts to close words (e.g., "cup" with a soft final /p/). Accuracy remained emerging, but there were more recognizable final consonants compared to last session. Visual cue of "tapping" the end sound was helpful.

3. Initial /k/ in isolation and syllables
- Practiced /k/ in isolation, then in CV syllables. Child produced a clearer /k/ sound in isolation several times with tongue-back cues and mirror. In syllables ("koo," "key"), still often substituted /t/, but there were a few accurate productions with immediate imitation.

4. 2–3 word phrases with clearer key words
- In play, focused on 2–3 word phrases that highlighted target words (e.g., "my cup," "big cat"). Child imitated clearer versions with support and occasionally initiated slightly clearer productions when prompted to "say it slowly." Intelligibility was still reduced, but there were more noticeable attempts to correct when SLP modeled.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Continue current targets; child is showing small but positive changes, particularly with attempts at final consonants and /k/ in isolation.
- Encourage caregiver to continue using slow, clear models and gentle reminders ("use your end sound") during everyday routines.
- Next session, consider introducing a small core set of CVC words for repetitive practice if progress with final consonants continues.
notes/2/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver reported that child has started to say "cat" and "cup" more clearly when reminded. Noted that child sometimes tries again if they are not understood, which is new. No concerns regarding hearing or health.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned smoothly and appeared comfortable in the therapy room. Maintained attention to structured games for slightly longer intervals than previous sessions. Accepted cues and corrections without frustration.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC words: /p, t, m/)
- Practiced a small set of CVC words ("cup," "cap," "cat," "hat," "ham," "mom"). Compared to last session, child showed increased consistency in attempting and sometimes marking final consonants. With visual tap and slow models, several words were produced with clearly audible end sounds. Accuracy is still not fully consistent but is clearly improving.

2. Initial /k/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Continued drills for /k/ using mirror, tongue-back cues, and "cough" trick. Child produced /k/ in isolation with moderate success, then moved into CV syllables ("koo," "kee," "kah"). There was a slight increase in accurate /k/ productions in syllables compared to last week, though substitutions to /t/ are still common. Child sometimes self-corrected after hearing SLP's model.

3. Syllable shapes (CVCV, alternating)
- Targeted alternating CVCV sequences (e.g., "bunny," "teddy," "kitty"). Child was more successful marking both syllables and less likely to reduce to single syllables. Some consonants and vowels were still distorted, but rhythm and syllable count were more accurate.

4. Short phrases with target words
- Used simple play-based routines (feeding animals, cars, etc.) to elicit short phrases such as "my cat," "big cup," "mom ham." When provided a clear model and cue to "say all your sounds," child imitated phrases with improving clarity on target words, particularly those practiced as CVC.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Maintain focus on final consonants and /k/ in CV syllables; child is showing emerging improvement and benefits from consistent visual and verbal cueing.
- Encourage caregiver to practice a small set of CVC words at home (e.g., "cup," "cat," "hat," "mom") using slow, emphasized end sounds.
- Plan to introduce a few early /k/ words (e.g., "car," "cow") in imitation next session if /k/ in syllables remains stable.
notes/3/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver noticed that child is saying "cat" and "hat" more clearly without being reminded sometimes. Also reported a new word "car" that sounded clearer this week. Intelligibility with close family is "a bit better" per caregiver.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child entered room independently and was enthusiastic about familiar routines. Demonstrated better sustained attention to table-top tasks and responded well to simple visual timer cues. Cooperative with cues and enjoyed turn-taking games.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC words: /p, t, m/)
- Continued work with a set of CVC words. Child showed a noticeable increase in consistent final consonant marking with verbal model plus visual tap. Several words ("cup," "hat," "mom") were produced with clearly audible final sounds in most trials. Less need for repeated modeling; child sometimes corrected when SLP highlighted the missing end sound.

2. Initial /k/ at syllable and word level
- Practiced /k/ in CV syllables and introduced a few simple words ("car," "cow," "key") in imitation. Child produced /k/ in syllables with improved accuracy and was able to imitate /k/ in initial position in several words with moderate cueing. There were still occasional /t/ substitutions, but overall consistency and ease of production improved from previous sessions.

3. CVCV and multisyllabic practice
- Used play themes to practice 2-syllable words ("bunny," "baby," "teddy") and a few early 3-syllable words ("banana" with support). Child more reliably produced both syllables, and caregiver was shown how to tap or clap for each part. Consonant accuracy was variable but syllable marking was stronger.

4. Short phrases with target CVC words
- Built short phrases around target CVC and /k/ words (e.g., "my car," "big cup," "hat on"). In imitation, child produced phrases with improved clarity on the key word and slightly better pacing. Some spontaneous attempts at similar phrases were heard in play, though not yet consistently clear.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Given the moderate gains, continue to expand CVC word list and practice initial /k/ at word level in structured play.
- Encourage caregiver to incorporate target words into daily routines (e.g., "cup" at snack, "hat" when going outside, "car" during play), modeling clear end sounds and initial /k/.
- Next session, consider probing simple carrier phrases ("I see ___") with familiar CVC words if accuracy remains steady.
notes/4/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver reported that family members are understanding child "a little more" during routine activities. Child is using words like "car," "cup," and "hat" frequently at home, though clarity still varies. Caregiver has been trying the end-sound tapping strategy.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned smoothly and eagerly requested preferred toys. Overall engagement remained good, with brief periods of distraction that responded to redirection. Child appeared proud when praised for clear words.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m/ and a few /k/)
- Continued practice with familiar CVC words and introduced a few with final /k/ (e.g., "back," "book" in imitation). Child maintained improved consistency for final /p, t, m/ from last session. Final /k/ was more challenging and often reduced or changed, though child attempted to mark an ending when cued.

2. Initial /k/ at word level
- Practiced initial /k/ in words like "car," "cow," "key," and "cup" using visual and verbal cues. Child continued to produce /k/ with moderate success in imitation, similar to last session, but needed ongoing reminders to "use the back sound." Some self-corrections were observed after SLP modeled.

3. CVCV and early multisyllabic words
- Targeted 2-syllable words ("bunny," "baby," "kitty") in play. Child reliably produced two syllables with support. Accuracy for individual consonants remained mixed but generally stable compared to last session.

4. Short phrases with target words
- Practiced short phrases ("my car," "more cup," "big hat") in structured play. Child imitated phrases with reasonable clarity on the key words when given a direct model. Independent phrases were less clear but similar to previous session.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Skills appear to be consolidating at the current level. Continue reinforcing final consonants, especially adding in a few final /k/ words, and maintain practice of initial /k/ in words.
- Caregiver encouraged to keep using the tapping strategy for end sounds and to model back-of-the-tongue /k/ (e.g., exaggerating "car," "cookie").
- Next session, plan to recheck consistency and consider gentle expansion of /k/ word list if performance remains steady.
notes/5/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver stated that child is "really trying" to be understood and sometimes repeats words when not understood. Noted that "car" and "cup" are often clear now, though multi-word sentences are still hard to understand.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child arrived in good spirits and participated willingly. Attention span for table tasks was similar to previous sessions, with need for occasional movement breaks. Responded well to specific praise for clear speech.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Revisited familiar CVC words and added a few more with final /k/ ("back," "bike"). Child maintained consistent final /p, t, m" with cues, and showed slightly more frequent attempts at final /k" though accuracy remained variable. Overall performance was comparable to last week with small fluctuations.

2. Initial /k/ at word level
- Practiced initial /k/ words ("car," "cow," "cookie," "key"). Child continued to require modeling and tongue-back cues but produced accurate /k/ in many imitated attempts. Occasional /t/ substitutions persisted. Performance was similar to prior session, with no major change.

3. CVCV and simple 3-syllable words
- Used play with animals and food to elicit 2-syllable words ("bunny," "tiger") and trial a few 3-syllable words ("banana" with strong support). Child continued to mark two syllables consistently and sometimes approximated all three in "banana" with clapping cues. Accuracy was generally stable.

4. Short phrases with target words
- Focused on 2–3 word phrases ("my car," "more cookie," "big cup"). Child imitated phrases with similar clarity to last week and occasionally initiated them during play. Intelligibility within these phrases is improving gradually but remains inconsistent.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Current week appears to be consolidating gains rather than showing large changes, which is appropriate at this stage.
- Continue with current target set for final consonants and initial /k/, emphasizing consistent practice and gentle self-monitoring ("did you use your end sound?").
- Caregiver advised to keep modeling and praising clear words/phrases at home during natural routines.
notes/6/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver reported that child is starting to be better understood by familiar adults and older siblings. Mentioned that child sometimes says "my car" and "more cookie" clearly without prompting. No new concerns.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned easily and engaged quickly with familiar games. Demonstrated slightly longer sustained attention for drill-like tasks when embedded in play (e.g., car races for each correct word). Cooperative and responsive to feedback.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Practiced a variety of CVC words in play ("cup," "hat," "ham," "back," "book"). Child showed generally stable accuracy for final /p, t, m" with ongoing visual and verbal cues. Final /k" remained more challenging but there were a few clearer productions of "back" and "book" in imitation. Overall pattern is slowly improving but similar to last week.

2. Initial /k/ at word level and early phrases
- Focused on initial /k/ words and then added them into short phrases ("my car," "blue car," "cookie please"). Child imitated initial /k" more consistently today, especially in high-interest words ("car," "cookie"). Still needed reminders to "use the back sound," but self-corrections were seen in a few trials after SLP's model.

3. CVCV and early multisyllabic words
- Continued practice of 2-syllable words ("bunny," "tiger," "cookie"). Child reliably produced both syllables with models and clapping. A few approximations of 3-syllable words like "banana" were heard with all syllables attempted, though consonant clarity varied.

4. 2–3 word phrases with target words
- In structured play, child imitated and sometimes spontaneously produced short phrases featuring target words ("my car," "more cookie," "big cup"). Intelligibility for these practiced phrases is improving, with clearer key words and slightly better overall pacing. Still needs support for longer, less familiar phrases.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Maintain focus on consolidating accurate final consonants and initial /k" in words and short phrases, as child is showing gradual but meaningful improvement.
- Encourage caregiver to highlight and repeat successful phrases at home (e.g., "my car," "more cookie") and praise clear attempts.
- Next session, plan to gently increase the variety of /k/ words and consider probing early carrier phrases ("I see ___") if accuracy allows.
notes/7/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver noted a "big difference" in how clearly child is saying certain words and short phrases, especially "my car," "more cookie," and "big cup." Reported that extended family commented that child was easier to understand during a recent visit.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child arrived cheerful and eager to participate. Tolerated longer periods of structured practice embedded in games. Demonstrated good turn-taking and responded to feedback with enthusiasm, often requesting "my turn" for more practice.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Practiced a broader set of CVC words ("cup," "cap," "hat," "ham," "back," "book," "bike"). Child produced final /p, t, m" with high consistency in imitation and some carryover into spontaneous play. Final /k" showed noticeable improvement; several clear productions of "back" and "book" were heard with only light cues. Fewer omissions and more consistent attempts to close words.

2. Initial /k/ at word and phrase level
- Expanded /k/ word list ("car," "cow," "cookie," "key," "coat"). Child imitated initial /k" accurately in most attempts with reduced cueing compared to previous sessions. Began to use clear /k" in short, structured phrases ("my car," "cookie please," "blue car") with good success when given a model. A few spontaneous phrases with accurate /k" were observed in play.

3. CVCV and simple 3-syllable words
- Targeted 2-syllable and early 3-syllable words in imitation ("bunny," "tiger," "banana," "pumpkin"). Child consistently marked two syllables and produced all three syllables in "banana" in several trials with clapping cues. Some consonant and vowel distortions remained, but rhythm and syllable structure were much more consistent.

4. Carrier phrases with target words (emerging)
- Introduced simple carrier phrases such as "I see ___" and "I want ___" using familiar CVC and /k/ words. With modeling and visual sentence strips, child imitated several 3–4 word phrases with recognizable key words and reasonably clear structure. Support was still required, but this represents a step up in complexity.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Given the moderate, meaningful gains, continue to build on final consonants and initial /k" at word and short-phrase levels, and further explore carrier phrases.
- Caregiver encouraged to use simple repetitive carrier phrases at home (e.g., "I want cookie," "I see car") and to praise clear productions.
- Next sessions will aim to increase independence in producing these phrases and begin to fade visual supports as appropriate.
notes/8/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver reported that child is now regularly using phrases like "I want cookie" and "my car" at home. Family notes that most familiar listeners understand child during routine activities, though longer sentences are still challenging.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child transitioned without difficulty and participated well in all activities. Attention and cooperation were similar to last week. Enjoyed using picture cards and sentence strips.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Continued practice with a mix of familiar and slightly newer CVC words. Child maintained strong accuracy for final /p, t, m" in imitation and short phrases. Final /k" remained improved, with consistent marking in common words like "back" and "book" when prompted. Overall performance was stable relative to last session.

2. Initial /k/ at word and phrase level
- Practiced initial /k" words and phrases ("my car," "cookie please," "key in"). Child continued to produce /k" with good consistency in imitation and needed only occasional cues to avoid /t/ substitutions. Accuracy and cueing needs were similar to prior session.

3. CVCV and simple multisyllabic words
- Targeted familiar 2- and 3-syllable words ("bunny," "tiger," "banana"). Child maintained ability to mark all syllables with clapping or tapping support. Articulation clarity within these words was comparable to last week, with some ongoing simplifications.

4. Carrier phrases and short sentences
- Used picture scenes to elicit 3–4 word carrier phrases ("I see a car," "I want big cookie"). With visual supports and a model, child imitated these phrases with generally clear target words. Independence in using these longer phrases remained limited; spontaneous speech was more typically 2–3 word combinations.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Skills are stable at the current level. Continue practicing final consonants and initial /k" in words and short phrases, aiming to gradually reduce the amount of modeling.
- Encourage caregiver to continue using and expanding simple carrier phrases at home and to reinforce attempts at longer utterances.
- Plan to monitor readiness for more independent phrase-level production over the next few sessions.
notes/9/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver stated that child continues to use clearer words and short phrases at home, especially with familiar routines. No major changes noted this week. Caregiver feels that progress is "holding steady."

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child was cooperative and engaged throughout the session. Participation in both play-based and structured tasks was similar to previous week. Responded well to routine structure and praise.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Revisited a range of CVC words. Child continued to produce final /p, t, m" accurately in most imitated attempts and within short phrases. Final /k" remained reasonably consistent in familiar words, with occasional omissions when attention decreased. Overall pattern was comparable to last session.

2. Initial /k/ at word and phrase level
- Practiced /k" words and short phrases ("my car," "cookie please," "key in box"). Child maintained good accuracy for initial /k" with light cueing. Performance level and cueing needs were similar to the previous week.

3. Multisyllabic words (2–3 syllables)
- Continued work on 2- and 3-syllable words such as "bunny," "tiger," "banana." Child consistently marked all syllables with support and produced several intelligible approximations. No significant change in accuracy relative to last session.

4. Carrier phrases and short sentences
- Used picture-supported tasks to elicit short sentences (e.g., "I see a big car," "I want more cookie"). With visual prompts and modeling, child imitated these 3–4 word utterances with reasonable clarity on the target words. Spontaneous speech remained somewhat shorter and less clear, but similar to last week.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Skills appear stable and consolidating. Continue to provide practice opportunities at the current phrase/sentence level while maintaining focus on accurate final consonants and initial /k".
- Caregiver encouraged to keep using visual and verbal routines (e.g., repeating "I want ___" and "I see ___") during daily activities.
- Monitor for signs of increased independence and potential to fade supports over the next sessions.
notes/10/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver shared that child is still using clearer words and phrases at home and is "holding steady" in terms of intelligibility. No new concerns or major changes reported this week.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child entered readily and participated in familiar routines without difficulty. Engagement and attention were comparable to prior sessions. Enjoyed repetitive practice games and responded positively to specific feedback.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Practiced a variety of CVC words in games and picture naming. Child continued to show strong accuracy for final /p, t, m" in imitation and short phrases. Final /k" remained relatively consistent in known words (e.g., "back," "book"), with some variability when new words were introduced. Overall skill level similar to last session.

2. Initial /k/ at word and phrase level
- Targeted initial /k" words within short phrases ("my car," "blue car," "cookie please"). Child maintained good accuracy with light verbal reminders. No notable change in cueing level or performance compared to previous week.

3. Multisyllabic words (2–3 syllables)
- Continued practice of 2- and 3-syllable words ("bunny," "tiger," "banana," "pumpkin"). Child reliably marked syllables when supported with clapping or tapping. Clarity of individual sounds remained similar to prior sessions, with ongoing simplification patterns.

4. Carrier phrases and short sentences
- Used familiar picture sets to elicit short sentences ("I see a big car," "I want more cookie," "I see a bunny"). With visual and verbal models, child imitated these phrases at a level consistent with last session. Spontaneous use of longer phrases occurred occasionally in play but remained less clear.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Current note reflects continued consolidation at this level. Maintain practice of final consonants and initial /k" within short phrases and simple sentences.
- Encourage caregiver to continue providing frequent, low-pressure opportunities for child to use practiced words and phrases at home.
- Plan to review overall progress at next session and discuss future therapy focus and home programming with caregiver.
notes/11/note_text:
Parent report:
- Caregiver reported that child continues to be better understood by familiar listeners and that routines using phrases like "I want ___" and "I see ___" are going well at home. No significant new changes this week.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Child was comfortable and engaged throughout the session. Participation in both structured and play-based tasks remained steady. Tolerated repetition well and seemed to enjoy demonstrating "good talking" for caregiver at the end of session.

Goals / Observations:
1. Final consonant production (CVC: /p, t, m, k/)
- Reviewed CVC words with final /p, t, m, k" across a mix of picture naming and play. Child continued to produce final /p, t, m" accurately in most contexts and showed generally consistent marking of final /k" in familiar words, with occasional omissions. Performance remains stable at this improved level.

2. Initial /k/ at word and phrase level
- Practiced initial /k" in words and short phrases ("my car," "cookie please," "key in box"). Child maintained good accuracy in imitation with light cueing. Occasional /t" substitutions were heard when child was more excited or speaking quickly, but overall pattern similar to previous weeks.

3. Multisyllabic words (2–3 syllables)
- Continued work on 2- and 3-syllable words ("bunny," "tiger," "banana," "pumpkin"). Child reliably produced correct syllable number with supports and produced several intelligible approximations. No major shift in accuracy compared to prior sessions.

4. Carrier phrases and short sentences
- Used familiar sentence frames ("I see a ___," "I want more ___") with target words. With visual prompts and models, child imitated 3–4 word utterances consistently and produced some similar phrases spontaneously in play. Intelligibility of these practiced phrases remained comparable to previous weeks.

Plan / Recommendations:
- Skills appear stable at the current level, with maintained gains in final consonant use and initial /k" productions. Continued therapy would focus on increasing independence, expanding to less familiar words, and supporting carryover into more spontaneous conversation.
- Caregiver encouraged to keep using clear models, simple carrier phrases, and praise for self-corrections at home.
- Recommend ongoing monitoring of generalization in natural settings and collaboration with preschool staff (if applicable) to support speech clarity across environments.

notes/0/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother reported that speech is "still pretty hard to understand" for unfamiliar listeners. She notices he leaves off sounds at the ends of words and often says "doo" for "shoe" and "tup" for "cup." No major changes since initial evaluation.

Behaviour/engagement:
- 4-year-old boy transitioned with mild hesitation but separated from mother with reassurance. Once in the room, he engaged well with cars and animal toys. Attention span was fair; he required occasional redirection back to tasks but responded to visual schedules and turn-taking games.

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ at syllable and word level
- Baseline probing completed. In isolation, he could approximate /k/ in 4/10 attempts with maximal verbal and visual cues ("tongue in the back" and mirror). In simple CV syllables ("ku, ki, ko"), accuracy decreased (2–3/10), with frequent fronting to /t/ (e.g., "too" for "coo"). At the word level ("car, key, cow"), he produced mostly /t/ substitutions in imitation. Requires direct modeling plus tactile cue on throat.

2. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- Probed final consonant production in imitation using picture cards ("cup, cat, home, map, boat"). Frequently omitted final consonants, producing open syllables ("cu" for "cup," "ca" for "cat"). With simultaneous production and exaggerated clinician model, he marked the final sound in ~2/10 trials across sounds. Greater success with final /m/ in "home" and "ham" when whispered first.

3. Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Attempted /s/ in isolation with visual cue (smile, teeth together, long airflow). He produced a mix of /d/ and /th/ substitutions, with occasional brief /s/-like frication when directly shaping from a long /t/ ("tssss"). Able to sustain an approximated /s/ in isolation in 3/10 attempts with maximal verbal and gestural cues. Not yet consistent in CV syllables.

4. Functional communication / spontaneous language sample
- During play, produced mainly 2–3-word phrases ("my car," "I want that," "no mine"). Intelligibility reduced at the sentence level due to sound errors and omissions. Able to repair breakdowns occasionally when prompted ("tell me again").

Plan/recommendations:
- Continue to establish accurate /k/ in isolation and simple syllables before expanding to more word practice.
- Continue targeting final consonants (p, t, m) in high-interest words using simultaneous production and tactile cues.
- Introduce parent to "whisper the last sound" strategy for final consonants during book reading.
- Provide caregiver with a short list of practice words for /k/ and final /m/ at the imitation level.
notes/1/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother reported that he has started trying to copy her when she emphasizes "back sounds" at home, especially in the car ("car, cookie"). She notices he sometimes says "tar" for "car" but is "trying hard." No concerns with hearing or health this week.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Arrived cheerful and eager to show therapist his toy truck. Transitioned smoothly into the therapy room. Maintained attention well for table-top games when they were brief and alternated with movement activities. Responded well to praise and sticker rewards.

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Marked improvement compared to last session. In isolation, he produced /k/ with clearer back placement in approximately 7/10 trials with moderate cues (mirror, "cough" trick). In CV syllables ("ku, ki, ko"), accuracy increased to about 6/10 in imitation; remaining attempts showed fronting to /t/. Able to self-correct occasionally when given a reminder to "use your back sound."

2. Initial /k/ in simple words (car, key, cow, cookie)
- Introduced imitated word level. With a direct model and visual cue, he produced initial /k/ accurately in ~5/10 trials. Best accuracy with "car" and "cow," more difficulty with "cookie." Errors were primarily /t/ substitutions. Needed moderate verbal cueing and slowed rate.

3. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- Continued work at imitation level with picture cards and a tap-on-the-table cue for the "last sound." Accuracy increased modestly: he marked final consonants in about 4/10 opportunities across sounds, with more consistent final /m/ ("ham," "home") than /p/ or /t/. Often added a short schwa after the consonant ("cup-uh").

4. Initial /s/ in isolation
- Practiced /s/ in front of the mirror using a "snake sound" visual. Able to hold an approximated /s/ for 4–5 seconds in about half of trials with significant clinician support. Still frequently shifts to /th/ or a stopped /t/ without cues.

Plan/recommendations:
- Continue to build consistency for /k/ in syllables and high-frequency words; maintain at the imitated word level next session.
- Maintain focus on final /m/ and /p/ using tapping cues and whispering the last sound.
- Encourage caregiver to practice 5–6 /k/ words daily (car, cow, key, cookie) using clear, slow models and praise for close approximations.
- Continue shaping /s/ in isolation before advancing to syllables.
notes/2/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother shared that he is now saying "car" with a clearer /k/ at home when reminded. She notes that he will sometimes correct himself ("tar... car!"). She has not yet noticed changes in final sounds but feels he is "more chatty" overall.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Entered session with good energy and quickly engaged with a farm play set. Demonstrated good turn-taking and followed simple directions. Required occasional breaks for movement but returned to tasks with minimal prompting.

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ at imitated word level and emerging carrier phrases
- At the imitated word level ("car, key, cow, cookie, kite"), he produced initial /k/ accurately in approximately 8/10 trials with light verbal cues ("back sound"). Fronting to /t/ occurred less frequently and he independently attempted to fix it 3–4 times after hearing his own error. Briefly introduced simple carrier phrase "my ___" with /k/ words; he successfully produced /k/ in 4/8 phrase attempts with moderate cueing.

2. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- Continued at imitation level using game-based activities. Accuracy showed slight improvement: final consonants were present in about 5/10 opportunities. Final /m/ remained strongest ("home," "gum"), followed by /p/ ("cup"). Final /t/ still often omitted. He responded well to visual cueing (touching picture when saying the last sound).

3. Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Practiced /s/ in isolation, then in simple CV sequences ("see, soo"). In isolation, he produced a recognizable /s/ in ~6/10 attempts with moderate verbal and visual cues. In syllables, accuracy dropped slightly (about 4/10) with frequent /th/ substitutions. Able to imitate a longer /ts" blend as a stepping stone in a few trials.

4. Functional communication / intelligibility in play
- During play-based routines, used 3–4-word utterances ("I want more cars," "put it in there"). Intelligibility to clinician improved when familiar with context, though sound errors remained evident. Showed increased willingness to repeat when asked and accepted feedback positively.

Plan/recommendations:
- Continue to strengthen /k/ at the imitated word level and expand into simple carrier phrases with support.
- Maintain work on final /p, m/ in a limited set of functional words; consider introducing simple visual cue card for "last sound."
- Continue shaping /s" from "t" and practice in high-interest syllables ("see, soup").
- Encourage caregiver to model and praise self-corrections ("you fixed it to car—nice back sound!").
notes/3/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother reported that he is now using "car" and "cookie" with clearer /k/ more often without being asked, especially during play. She still finds it hard to hear final sounds, but she feels "some words are clearer." No new concerns.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Transitioned smoothly and checked the visual schedule independently. Participated well in structured games and was able to stay with table-top tasks for slightly longer intervals. Needed gentle reminders to speak slowly when excited.

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ at word and carrier phrase level
- At the imitated word level, he produced initial /k/ with good accuracy (~9/10 trials) using only occasional verbal reminders. Began to practice short carrier phrases ("my car," "big cookie," "go cow") in structured play. In these phrases, initial /k/ remained accurate in approximately 7/10 attempts, with breakdowns occurring when he spoke quickly. Demonstrated a few spontaneous /k/ words in play ("car go," "cow there") without prompts.

2. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- Continued at the imitated word level. Final /m/ was present in most trials (about 7/10) for target words ("home, gum, ham"). Final /p/ accuracy increased modestly to ~6/10 with tapping cue; he still occasionally produced "cu" for "cup." Final /t/ remained the most challenging, with frequent omissions or substitutions ("ca" for "cat").

3. Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- In isolation, he produced a clear /s/ in roughly 7/10 attempts with a visual snake cue, needing less physical prompting. In CV syllables ("see, soo, sah"), accuracy hovered around 5/10. Some productions were distorted but moving closer to target. He tolerated repeated practice and enjoyed the "snake sound" game.

4. Early generalization / self-monitoring
- When presented with picture choices, he occasionally commented on his own speech ("I did back sound") after producing /k/. Needed adult support to identify when final sounds were missing.

Plan/recommendations:
- Continue /k/ in carrier phrases and begin to probe for spontaneous use in more natural play.
- Maintain focus on final /p, m/ and begin to isolate a small set of final /t/ words for intensive practice.
- Continue building stability of /s/ in syllables before moving to word level.
- Encourage caregiver to practice short phrases with /k/ ("my car," "big cookie") during daily routines and to gently emphasize final sounds in a few chosen words.
notes/4/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother shared that he is now often saying "my car" and "big cookie" clearly at home, and grandparents commented that he is a "little easier to understand." She still notices dropping of final sounds, especially in fast speech. No changes in health or routine.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Arrived in good spirits and quickly engaged with a board game. Demonstrated improved ability to follow multi-step directions within activities. Occasionally rushed through words when excited, but accepted cues to "use slow talking."

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ in carrier phrases and emerging short sentences
- In structured play, he produced /k/ accurately in carrier phrases ("my car," "go cow," "big cookie") in approximately 9/10 imitated opportunities with minimal cueing. Began to use short 3–4-word sentences ("my car go fast") in imitation; /k/ remained mostly accurate within these contexts (~7/10). Several spontaneous uses of /k/ words ("car," "cow," "cookie") observed without prompting.

2. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- Continued practice at the imitated word level using picture matching and a "tap the last sound" strategy. Final /m/ remained strong (~8/10) and final /p/ showed gradual improvement (~7/10). Final /t/ continued to be inconsistent (approximately 4/10), with frequent omissions. He responded well when final /t/ was highlighted in a whisper ("caT").

3. Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Produced /s/ in isolation with good consistency (~8/10) using only a light visual cue. In CV syllables, accuracy increased slightly to about 6/10. Some productions had minor distortions but were clearly different from /t/ or /th/. He enjoyed taking turns being the "snake" and giving the clinician feedback.

4. Functional communication / intelligibility
- During free play, used 3–5-word phrases to comment and request ("I want more cars," "put it in there," "look my car"). Intelligibility to clinician was fair, with clearer /k/ helping in familiar words. Final consonant omissions and /s/ distortions still impacted overall clarity.

Plan/recommendations:
- Continue strengthening /k/ in short sentences and monitor spontaneous use across activities.
- Maintain targeted practice of final /p, m/ and continue focused work on a small set of final /t/ words (e.g., cat, hat, boat).
- Prepare to introduce /s/ at the imitated word level in the next 1–2 sessions if syllable-level accuracy remains steady.
- Encourage caregiver to praise clear /k/ and final consonants, and to model slow, clear speech during daily routines.
notes/5/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother noted that he is now starting to use clearer /k/ words even when playing independently (e.g., heard him say "my car go" to himself). She still finds that some words end "too early" without the last sound. She has been practicing a few final /t/ words but feels he "forgets" them quickly.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Transitioned easily and requested preferred toys verbally. Stayed engaged in structured tasks for most of the session with minimal redirection. Some mild silliness when he became tired near the end, but he remained cooperative.

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ in short sentences and spontaneous play
- In imitation, he produced /k/ accurately in 3–5-word sentences ("my car goes fast," "I see a cow") in about 8–9/10 opportunities with minimal verbal cues. During unstructured play, several spontaneous /k/ productions were observed ("car go," "cow there," "cookie please") with good accuracy. Occasional fronting to /t/ occurred when speaking quickly, but he often accepted a prompt to repeat using his "back sound."

2. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- At the imitated word level, final /m/ remained stable at a high accuracy (~8/10), and final /p/ stayed around 7/10 with tapping cues. Final /t/ showed slight growth but remained inconsistent (~5/10). He was more successful in slow, exaggerated productions and when the clinician segmented the word ("ca...t").

3. Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Continued practice of /s/ in isolation and CV syllables. Isolation remained strong (~8/10) with minimal cueing. In syllables, accuracy held around 6–7/10, with fewer clear /th/ substitutions. Some productions were still somewhat slushy, but airflow was more continuous.

4. Functional communication / self-advocacy
- He used phrases to ask for help and more turns ("I want more," "help me," "my turn now"). When not understood, he accepted cues to "say it again" and occasionally added gestures for clarification.

Plan/recommendations:
- Maintain /k/ practice within short sentences and encourage more spontaneous use in play scenarios.
- Continue targeted sets for final /p, t, m" with emphasis on slow rate and tapping the last sound.
- If /s/ syllable accuracy remains stable, plan to introduce a small set of /s/ words in imitation next session.
- Encourage caregiver to keep sessions short and fun at home, focusing on a few chosen words for final /t/ and praising all attempts.
notes/6/note_text:
Parent/caregiver report:
- Mother reported that his "car" and "cookie" words are now "almost always clear" and that he uses them with cousins when playing. She still does not notice many final sounds unless she reminds him. She has not seen much change yet in his "snake sound" (/s/) in words.

Behaviour/engagement:
- Entered the room independently and checked the schedule. Participation was good; he followed routines and accepted therapist-selected activities. Needed a few prompts to slow down his speech when excited.

Goals and observations:
1. Initial /k/ in sentences and conversation
- At the sentence level in imitation (3–5 words), /k/ remained accurate in approximately 9/10 opportunities with minimal cues. In structured conversation about pictures, he used /k/ correctly in familiar words ("car, cookie, cow") most of the time. Occasional fronting persisted in longer or less familiar words but did not significantly impact his ability to participate.

2. Final consonant production (p, t, m) in CVC words
- Continued focus at the imitated word level with game-based tasks. Accuracy for final /m/ and /p/ remained similar to last week (~8/10 and ~7/10 respectively) with supports. Final /t/ accuracy was variable, around 5/10, with better productions when he had a visual and tactile cue. No consistent carryover to spontaneous speech yet.

3. Initial /s/ in isolation and CV syllables
- Practiced /s/ again in isolation and CV syllables. Performance was stable: isolation ~8/10 with light cues; syllable-level ~6/10 with some distortions. Continued to require focused attention to maintain airflow and tongue placement.

4. Functional communication
- Used 3–5-word phrases across activities. Intelligibility improved for familiar topics, largely due to stronger /k/ productions, but reduced final consonants and /s/ distortions continue to affect clarity in unfamiliar contexts.

Plan/recommendations:
- Continue consolidating /k/ in sentence-level tasks and monitor for generalization in less structured conversation.
- Maintain practice of final /p, t, m" at the word level; consider adding simple phrases ("my cup," "big cat") for /p/ and /m/ if accuracy remains steady.
- Continue /s/ at the syllable level; hold off on advancing to words until productions are more consistent.
